Content_Sketch: Underground bunker at night — screens showing coordinates of nuclear facilities
Illustration_Note: Dark bunker interior, blue-lit screens showing satellite maps, stickman silhouette at head of table

Content_Sketch: Your hand on the red folder. 50 years to reach this moment
Illustration_Note: Close-up of stickman hand resting on red classified folder, table edge visible

Content_Sketch: Everyone in the room waiting for you to speak. You haven't yet
Illustration_Note: War room table with multiple faceless stickmen in uniforms looking toward subject at head

Content_Sketch: Born in Middle Eastern capital — family of intellectuals obsessed with history
Illustration_Note: Small stickman child in apartment filled with bookshelves, father figure with glasses reading

Content_Sketch: Father teaches history: your people survive because they NEVER trust the world is safe
Illustration_Note: Father stickman at table pointing at book, child stickman listening, dark room with single lamp

Content_Sketch: Age 14 — family moves to American suburb in Philadelphia
Illustration_Note: Medium stickman teen on suburban American street, houses with lawns, school bus in background

Content_Sketch: Teenager between two worlds — America and home
Illustration_Note: Split frame: left=suburban school, right=Middle Eastern city skyline, teen stickman in center

Content_Sketch: Age 18 — returns home, joins elite commando unit
Illustration_Note: Tall-medium stickman in military uniform with beret, training ground with obstacle course

Content_Sketch: Training: shooting, breaching doors, learning that hesitation = death
Illustration_Note: Stickman in commando gear breaching a door, rifle raised, dust/debris

Content_Sketch: 1972 — shot in the shoulder during hijacked airplane rescue on tarmac
Illustration_Note: Airport tarmac at night, stickman near airplane stairs, flash of gunfire, shoulder wound indicated

Content_Sketch: First scar. Not the last
Illustration_Note: Close-up stickman touching shoulder, military uniform, dark background

Content_Sketch: 1976 — your brother leads commando team to rescue hostages in East Africa
Illustration_Note: Airport terminal at night, brother stickman in beret leading armed team toward building

Content_Sketch: Mission succeeds. Your brother dies. He was 30
Illustration_Note: Brother stickman silhouette falling, bright flash behind, team emerging with hostages

Content_Sketch: You receive the call in America. The world changes in one phone call
Illustration_Note: Young adult stickman holding phone, American apartment, frozen posture

Content_Sketch: Brother's photograph — from now on it follows you for 50 years
Illustration_Note: Framed photograph of brother in military beret on desk, stickman hand touching frame

Content_Sketch: Enter diplomacy — deputy ambassador, then ambassador to the UN in America
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman in suit at podium with flags, large assembly hall, faceless audience

Content_Sketch: UN speech in perfect English — your strongest weapon isn't a gun
Illustration_Note: Stickman at UN podium, microphone, gesturing, large projected screen behind

Content_Sketch: Age 46 — youngest leader ever elected in your country
Illustration_Note: Stickman at victory rally podium, confetti, flags, crowd of faceless stickmen

Content_Sketch: First term: short, chaotic, election defeat 1999
Illustration_Note: Same podium now empty, lights off, single stickman walking away

Content_Sketch: 10 years in political wilderness. Everyone thinks you're finished
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone in modest office, newspaper clippings on wall, brother's photo still there

Content_Sketch: You don't think so. You've never thought so
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk, reading glasses, red folder, jaw set, dark room

Content_Sketch: 2009 — back in the leader's chair. No one could stop you
Illustration_Note: Stickman entering grand office, generals lining hallway, saluting

Content_Sketch: Address American Congress uninvited — opposing nuclear deal with the enemy
Illustration_Note: Stickman at US Congress podium, packed chamber of faceless lawmakers, standing ovation from half

Content_Sketch: Hold up bomb diagram at UN General Assembly — entire world watching
Illustration_Note: Stickman at UN podium holding up large diagram showing bomb with red line drawn on it

Content_Sketch: The regional adversary is everything. It always was. Since the day your brother died
Illustration_Note: Stickman staring at wall map with enemy country highlighted, brother's photo visible on desk

Content_Sketch: Three marriages. The third wife — loved and controversial
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen (subject + wife) at formal event, cameras flashing, faceless press

Content_Sketch: Court summons: bribery, fraud, breach of trust — three cases at once
Illustration_Note: Stickman in courtroom, three prosecutors with folders, judge bench above

Content_Sketch: You say: political persecution. Half the country believes you. Half doesn't
Illustration_Note: Split frame: supporters with flags on left, protesters with signs on right, stickman in center at podium

Content_Sketch: October morning. Dawn. Everything shatters
Illustration_Note: Stickman jolted awake by phone, bedroom, early morning light, screens flickering on

Content_Sketch: 1,200 dead. The security system YOU built — collapsed
Illustration_Note: Stickman in bunker watching multiple screens showing attack footage, hands on table

Content_Sketch: They ask: who is responsible? You don't answer
Illustration_Note: Press conference podium, stickman at microphone, cameras, silence

Content_Sketch: War begins. Multiple fronts. You command from the bunker
Illustration_Note: Bunker war room, maps on screens, stickman at head of table with generals around

Content_Sketch: The world condemns. Allies pull away. You don't stop
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone at desk, phone off hook, newspaper headlines on screen, empty chairs

Content_Sketch: June 2025. Solo strikes on the enemy's nuclear facilities
Illustration_Note: Night sky, missile trails, satellite view of target facility, stickman watching from bunker screen

Content_Sketch: You gave the order alone. Didn't wait for allies. Didn't ask permission
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing at war room table, phone in hand, red folder open, generals watching

Content_Sketch: The world wakes up to the news: enrichment facility destroyed
Illustration_Note: Morning news screens across multiple locations, satellite damage imagery, world map

Content_Sketch: 50 years since your brother died. This is the moment you've been waiting for
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk, red folder closed, brother's photo beside it, dawn light through bunker slit

Content_Sketch: But it's not enough. It's never enough
Illustration_Note: Same frame, stickman's hand moving toward phone again

Content_Sketch: Request presidential pardon for corruption cases — while commanding a war
Illustration_Note: Split frame: courtroom documents on left, war room screens on right, stickman seated between both

Content_Sketch: Protector of the nation or a man running from his own trial?
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk, half-shadowed face, red folder in one hand, legal document in other

Content_Sketch: February 28, 2026. Joint campaign with American ally begins
Illustration_Note: Two leaders' stickmen in joint war room, screens everywhere, flags of both nations

Content_Sketch: Largest military operation since 2003. Missiles, drones, nuclear facilities
Illustration_Note: Multiple missile trails across night sky, explosions at industrial facilities, drone silhouettes

Content_Sketch: Enemy supreme leader — eliminated at the start of the campaign
Illustration_Note: Breaking news screens in war room, stickmen looking at central screen, red alert indicators

Content_Sketch: You and the ally in the war room. Two leaders frighteningly alike
Illustration_Note: Two tall stickmen side by side at war table, same posture, same suit, screens reflecting on both

Content_Sketch: The strait closes. Oil spikes worldwide. The world holds its breath
Illustration_Note: Map showing closed strait, oil price graphics spiking, ships stopped, red zones

Content_Sketch: Retaliatory missiles hit allied bases across the region
Illustration_Note: Missile impacts on military base, explosions, faceless soldiers running, night sky lit up

Content_Sketch: Civilian casualties. The number depends on who's counting
Illustration_Note: Stickman at briefing podium, screen behind showing numbers, press cameras

Content_Sketch: Week six. The war has no end date
Illustration_Note: Calendar on wall with weeks crossed out, no end marked, bunker background

Content_Sketch: You are 76. In the bunker. Still the one deciding
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone at bunker desk, screens glowing, reading glasses on, red folder

Content_Sketch: Brother's photo on the desk. He is forever 30
Illustration_Note: Brother's framed photo on desk beside red folder, aged stickman hand near it

Content_Sketch: You've outlived him by nearly half a century
Illustration_Note: Same desk, wider angle showing aged stickman and young brother's photo side by side

Content_Sketch: Red folder still on the desk. Every day there's a new red folder
Illustration_Note: Desk with red folder, same composition as HOOK but now with brother's photo added

Content_Sketch: 50 years ago you were the younger brother. Now you're the one who decides
Illustration_Note: Split: young stickman receiving phone call (1976) vs aged stickman at war room (2026)

Content_Sketch: The distance between losing your brother and ordering the strike — that's your life
Illustration_Note: Timeline visual: 1976 to 2026, brother's photo morphing into red folder

Content_Sketch: You're still sitting there. Because no one else stayed long enough
Illustration_Note: Final wide shot: aged stickman alone in bunker, screens still on, red folder closed, brother's photo
